This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Thermopsis persica include isoflavones, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thermopsis persica is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, with its most notable preparations being used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate menopausal symptoms.
